message:
fix(trace): wire skipped_due_to_budget through TraceCollector

Complement the existing skipped_due_to_budget field in ExplainTrace by
adding runtime tracking support to TraceCollector:

## Changes

- Add `skipped_due_to_budget: bool` field to TraceCollector struct
- Initialize to `false` in TraceCollector::new()
- Add `set_budget_skip(&mut self, skipped: bool)` setter method
- Wire the field through to ExplainTrace in `finish()` method

## Context

The ExplainTrace struct already had a skipped_due_to_budget field (added in
commit dbe8a4d), but it was hardcoded to `false` in TraceCollector::finish().
This change allows the evaluator to properly set this flag when a performance
budget is exceeded, enabling explain mode to accurately report when deeper
analysis was skipped due to timing constraints.

## Affected Flow

1. Evaluator calls `trace.set_budget_skip(true)` when budget is exhausted
2. TraceCollector stores the value during evaluation
3. finish() propagates to ExplainTrace output
4. dcg explain shows user whether result may be incomplete

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
